title: dianthus collinus subsp. collinus
intro: Dianthus collinus subsp. collinus, also known as the alpine pink, is a perennial plant species in the carnation family (Caryophyllaceae) that is native to Europe and Western Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which make it effective in treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Dianthus collinus subsp. collinus include reducing inflammation, fighting off infections, and soothing digestive issues. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions, and it is also used as a garnish in salads and as a decorative plant in gardens.
